description: Optimize the core RAG pipeline, deduplication, and search workflows based on comprehensive codebase analysis and 2025-2026 industry best practices. Focus areas: chunking quality, dedup performance, similar paper matching, image/multimodal processing, and workflow parallelization.

userStories.title: Replace character-based chunk sizing with token-based sizing
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need my paper chunks to be sized by tokens (not characters) so that embeddings are consistent across languages and the RAG pipeline performs optimally. Currently chunk_size=1024 counts characters via len(), but token_count uses len(text.split()) (word count) -- these metrics are inconsistent and unreliable for CJK languages.
acceptanceCriteria: Use tiktoken (cl100k_base) for accurate token counting in all chunking functions
Default chunk_size is 512 tokens (not 1024 characters)
Default overlap is 50 tokens (not 100 words)
Token count in chunk metadata accurately reflects tiktoken count
chunk_mineru_markdown() uses token-based splitting
chunk_text() uses token-based splitting
marker-pdf fallback splits at token boundaries, not character boundaries
Test: verify a Chinese paragraph chunk has correct token count
Test: verify a 512-token English chunk has ~400-600 words
Test: verify overlap preserves last N tokens from previous chunk
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/services/ocr_service.py lines 329-544. Add tiktoken dependency to backend requirements.txt or pyproject.toml. Change chunk_size default from 1024 to 512 tokens. Replace len(text) with len(enc.encode(text)) for size checks. Replace len(text.split()) with len(enc.encode(text)) for token_count. For overlap: encode text, take last N tokens, decode back.

userStories.title: Add sentence-aware paragraph splitting to chunking
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need chunks to respect sentence boundaries so that RAG retrieval returns complete thoughts, not mid-sentence fragments. Currently _flush_text_chunk() splits on paragraph boundaries only -- long paragraphs are never subdivided, creating oversized chunks.
acceptanceCriteria: _flush_text_chunk() splits long paragraphs at sentence boundaries (sentence-ending punctuation)
No chunk exceeds token limit (512) even for very long paragraphs
Sentence splitting preserves the full sentence -- never cuts mid-sentence
Fallback path (chunk_text) also respects sentence boundaries
Test: verify a 2000-character paragraph is split into multiple chunks at sentence boundaries
Test: verify each split chunk starts and ends with complete sentences
Test: verify overlap includes full sentences at the boundary
userStories.notes: Add sentence splitting regex: r'(?<=[.。!?])\s+' for English/Chinese. In _flush_text_chunk, if a single paragraph exceeds chunk_size, split it into sentences first, then accumulate sentences into chunks.

userStories.title: Optimize dedup from O(N^2) to O(N) with token fingerprint pre-grouping
userStories.description: As a researcher with 1000+ papers, I need deduplication to complete in seconds, not minutes. Currently title_similarity_dedup and find_llm_dedup_candidates do O(N^2) pairwise SequenceMatcher comparisons -- for 1000 papers, that is ~500K comparisons.
acceptanceCriteria: title_similarity_dedup uses token-based fingerprint pre-grouping to reduce comparisons
find_llm_dedup_candidates uses the same fingerprint optimization
For 1000 papers, comparisons drop from ~500K to ~10K or fewer
Dedup results are identical to before
Performance improvement is measurable (timing logs added)
Test: verify dedup finds same duplicates on a project with known duplicates
Test: verify 1000 papers complete in under 5 seconds
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/services/dedup_service.py. Create title_fingerprint() function: normalize, remove stop words, sort tokens, create frozenset hash. Group papers by fingerprint. Only run SequenceMatcher within fingerprint groups. Add timing logs.

userStories.title: Eliminate PaddleOCR disk I/O by using in-memory image processing
userStories.description: As a researcher processing scanned PDFs, I need OCR to be fast. Currently PaddleOCR writes each page as a PNG to /tmp/ then reads it back -- unnecessary disk I/O. PaddleOCR accepts PIL Image objects or numpy arrays directly.
acceptanceCriteria: extract_text_ocr() passes PIL Image to PaddleOCR directly without writing to /tmp
No temporary PNG files created during OCR
OCR results are identical to before
Test: verify a scanned PDF is OCR'd correctly without temp files
Test: verify /tmp has no leftover OCR files after processing
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/services/ocr_service.py lines 156-203. Replace page.get_pixmap() -> PNG file -> ocr.ocr(file) with page.get_pixmap() -> Image.frombytes() -> ocr.ocr(image). Remove all os.remove(temp_file) calls.

userStories.title: Enable MinerU image extraction and store figure metadata
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need figures from papers to be extracted and indexed. Currently MinerU's return_images is hardcoded to 'false', so all figure/image content is discarded.
acceptanceCriteria: MinerU client sets return_images='true' in API payload
Extracted images are saved to project's figure storage directory
Figure paths are stored in chunk metadata (figure_path field already exists)
Test: verify a PDF processed with MinerU has figure files saved
Test: verify figure_path in chunk metadata points to a valid file
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/services/mineru_client.py line 63. Change 'return_images': 'false' to 'true'. Save images to /data0/djx/omelette/figures/{project_id}/paper_{paper_id}/.

userStories.title: Parallelize LangGraph OCR node with asyncio.gather
userStories.description: As a researcher uploading multiple PDFs, I want the LangGraph pipeline to process papers in parallel. Currently ocr_node processes papers sequentially with a for loop.
acceptanceCriteria: ocr_node processes multiple papers concurrently using asyncio.gather
GPU semaphore limits parallelism to available GPUs
OCR results are identical to sequential processing
Processing time for N papers scales as O(N/GPU_COUNT) instead of O(N)
Test: verify 3 PDFs process in parallel
Test: verify a single PDF failure doesn't crash the entire batch
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/pipelines/nodes.py lines 294-330. Replace sequential for loop with asyncio.gather(*[process_one(p) for p in papers], return_exceptions=True). Use GPU semaphore from config.

userStories.title: Batch DOI check for search import instead of N separate queries
userStories.description: As a researcher running discovery searches, I need search import to be fast. Currently the system does N separate DB queries to check if each DOI exists.
acceptanceCriteria: Search import fetches all existing DOIs in a single SQL query
Results are filtered in Python to identify new papers
Test: verify a search with 50 results checks DOIs in 1 query instead of 50
Test: verify import results match before
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/api/v1/search.py lines 58-73. Replace per-result DOI check with single batch query: select(Paper.doi).where(Paper.doi.in_(all_dois)).

userStories.title: Add weighted chunk averaging for similar paper matching
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need similar paper recommendations to be more accurate. Currently all chunks are averaged equally, giving references section the same weight as abstract and conclusion.
acceptanceCriteria: Similar paper endpoint uses weighted averaging: abstract=3x, intro=2x, conclusion=2.5x, methods=1x, results=1.5x, discussion=2x, references=0x
Median distance used instead of mean for robustness against outlier chunks
Test: verify abstract-heavy papers rank higher for similar-paper queries
Test: verify API response format is unchanged
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/api/v1/papers.py lines 362-456. Add SECTION_WEIGHTS dict. Use statistics.median instead of mean for distance aggregation. Fetch section info from chunk metadata.

userStories.title: Add cross-source deduplication to federated search results
userStories.description: As a researcher running discovery searches, I need the same paper to appear only once in results. Currently the federated search concatenates results from 4 providers without any cross-source dedup.
acceptanceCriteria: Search results are deduplicated across providers using DOI matching
Papers without DOI are deduplicated using normalized title matching
When duplicates found, the version with most complete metadata is kept
Test: verify a known paper found by 3 providers appears only once
Test: verify no papers are incorrectly deduplicated
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/services/search_service.py. Add _dedup_results() method after gather. Group by DOI, then fingerprint match for no-DOI papers.

userStories.title: Add PDF content hash for upload-time deduplication
userStories.description: As a researcher, I need to prevent uploading the exact same PDF twice with different filenames. Currently no content dedup check exists.
acceptanceCriteria: Upload endpoint computes SHA-256 hash of PDF content
Hash is stored in a new content_hash column on Paper model
Upload checks for existing papers with same content_hash
Duplicate upload is rejected with a clear error message
Alembic migration adds content_hash column
Test: verify uploading the same PDF twice returns a conflict error
userStories.notes: Files: backend/app/models/paper.py, backend/app/api/v1/upload.py. Use hashlib.sha256 on file content. Add UniqueConstraint on content_hash. Add Alembic migration.
